```json
[
  {
    "idea": "Weekly Google Trends Surge in Pest Control Searches",
    "family": "local_economy_weirdness",
    "data": {
      "source": "Google Trends weekly search volume for 'pest control near me' and 'termite inspection' through google_trends adapter",
      "type": "api",
      "frequency": "weekly",
      "stable": true
    },
    "behavior": "Spikes in pest control searches correlate with seasonal property stress and localized economic downturn; homeowners prioritize pest mitigation when neighborhood conditions deteriorate or rental markets tighten.",
    "market_impact": {
      "direction": "bearish",
      "sector": "XLRE",
      "rationale": "Rising pest control urgency signals deteriorating property conditions and neighborhood quality, pressuring real estate valuations and sentiment."
    },
    "trade_logic": {
      "entry": "When weekly Google Trends search volume for pest control queries rises 35% or more above the 8-week rolling average",
      "exit": "After 10 trading days or when search volume falls back to within 15% of rolling average",
      "expected_frequency": "medium"
    },
    "expected_fire_rate": "monthly",
    "historical_backfill": true,
    "minimum_history_months": 12,
    "adapter_status": "existing",
    "trigger_sensitivity": "high",
    "reason_it_will_fire_in_30_days": "Seasonal pest activity and neighborhood stress cycles generate regular search spikes; threshold is tight enough to fire 2–4 times per quarter."
  },
```
